data_flow_pipeline.entry_point: User input enters through command-line arguments parsed in src/bin/bat/main.rs::run(), which invokes the App parser to extract flags, files, and options

stages.responsibility: Parse command-line arguments, build configuration object with user preferences (theme, colors, paging, line ranges, etc.)
stages.output: Config struct containing all user settings and input file paths

stages.responsibility: Open and prepare input sources (files, stdin, preprocessed content). Handle encoding detection, binary content, and special file types
stages.input: File paths or stdin stream from Config
stages.output: OpenedInput with InputReader for reading file content line-by-line

stages.responsibility: Load syntax definitions and color themes. Detect appropriate syntax for input based on filename, extension, or first-line heuristics
stages.input: Input file metadata, user theme/syntax preferences from Config

stages.responsibility: Normalize line endings, handle UTF-16/UTF-8 decoding, expand tabs, detect and handle non-printable characters
stages.output: Normalized UTF-8 strings ready for processing

stages.responsibility: If in diff mode, compute git diff and mark changed lines. Filter visible lines based on user-specified ranges
stages.input: Preprocessed content, Config with line ranges and diff settings
stages.output: Filtered content with line change markers (added/removed/modified)

stages.responsibility: Apply syntax highlighting using tree-sitter/syntect. Generate ANSI color codes. Add line numbers, grid borders, and git change indicators
stages.input: Normalized content, syntax definition, theme, line metadata
stages.output: ANSI-formatted strings with colors, styles, and decorations

stages.responsibility: Write formatted output to terminal or pager. Handle wrapping, pagination, and terminal title updates
stages.output: Terminal display via stdout or pager process

data_flow_pipeline.exit_point: Output flows to stdout (direct or via less/pager) where the terminal renders the formatted, syntax-highlighted file content

main.rs.role: Entry point. Parses CLI args and orchestrates the main execution flow

app.rs.role: Defines command-line argument structure and parsing logic using clap

config.rs.role: Converts parsed CLI args into Config struct. Handles pager selection and environment variables

controller.rs.role: Main orchestrator. Coordinates input reading, printing, and output handling

input.rs.role: Handles file opening, encoding detection, and line-by-line reading with UTF-16/UTF-8 support

assets.rs.role: Manages syntax and theme assets. Detects syntax from filename, extension, or content

preprocessor.rs.role: Normalizes content: expands tabs, strips ANSI codes, handles non-printable characters

diff.rs.role: Computes git diffs and tracks line changes for diff mode display

line_range.rs.role: Parses and manages line range specifications. Filters visible lines based on user ranges

printer.rs.role: Core rendering engine. Applies syntax highlighting, generates ANSI codes, adds decorations

vscreen.rs.role: Virtual screen abstraction. Parses and manages ANSI escape sequences for proper styling

decorations.rs.role: Generates line number, grid border, and git change decorations

output.rs.role: Manages output destination (stdout, pager, file). Handles paging mode and output type selection

pager.rs.role: Spawns and communicates with pager processes (less, more, custom)

style.rs.role: Manages styling configuration and color scheme detection

pretty_printer.rs.role: High-level API for printing with fluent configuration. Wraps controller functionality

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Parses command-line arguments using clap-based App structure
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: ARGV -> parsed flags, options, input file paths

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Converts parsed args into Config object with all user preferences
Instantiates Controller with Config and HighlightingAssets, then calls run()
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Config, HighlightingAssets, input iterator -> execution results

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Opens input files and creates InputReader for line-by-line reading
Loads syntax definitions and themes; detects syntax for input
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Input metadata, user preferences -> HighlightingAssets with matched syntax and theme

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Computes git diff if diff mode is enabled
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: File path, Config -> HashMap of line changes

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Creates InteractivePrinter or SimplePrinter and calls print_line() for each line
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Content lines, syntax, theme, decorations -> ANSI-formatted output

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Creates OutputType from Config and writes formatted content to it
Normalizes line content before highlighting (expand tabs, strip ANSI, handle non-printable)
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Raw line bytes -> normalized UTF-8 string

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Parses ANSI escape sequences in highlighted content for proper wrapping and styling
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: ANSI-formatted string -> parsed escape sequence offsets

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Generates line numbers, grid borders, and git change markers
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Line number, change status -> formatted decoration string

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Detects and handles encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16) during line reading
Spawns pager process if paging mode is enabled
Uses InputReader to read first line for syntax detection heuristics
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: InputReader -> first line content for detection

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Filters which lines are visible based on user-specified ranges

extension_proposal.feature: Custom output formatter plugin system allowing users to define custom syntax highlighting rules or output formats
required_changes: Extend assets.rs to load custom syntax definitions from user config directory
Modify printer.rs to support pluggable highlighting backends beyond syntect
Extend output.rs to support custom output formatters (e.g., HTML, Markdown, JSON)
Add configuration parsing in config.rs for plugin paths and options
integration_points: After syntax detection in assets.rs, check for custom syntax definitions
In printer.rs::highlight_regions_for_line(), route to custom highlighter if available
In output.rs::from_mode(), instantiate custom formatter if specified in Config
In controller.rs::print_file_ranges(), pass output through custom formatter before writing
